response:
Sec. 1  
Allergies to peanuts are immune system responses to proteins found in peanuts  
These proteins trigger reactions when ingested or come into contact with the skin  
Living underwater does not eliminate exposure to these proteins  
People with peanut allergies can still consume peanuts or products containing them even in aquatic environments  
The presence of water does not alter the chemical structure of peanut proteins  
Therefore the allergic reaction can still occur if the person is exposed to peanuts  

Sec. 2  
Underwater living does not provide a protective barrier against allergens  
Allergic reactions are not affected by the surrounding medium  
The body's immune system reacts based on the presence of allergens not their location  
People with peanut allergies must avoid peanuts regardless of their environment  
Living underwater is not a solution for managing peanut allergies  
The primary concern is avoiding allergens not the physical setting
